ARGUED AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: DEFENDANT EXXON MOBIL CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. THE DECLARATION OF ROBERT MINGRAM CREATES A TRIABLE ISSUE WHETHER MR. MCGREW WAS EXPOSED TO ASBESTOS-CONTAINING INSULATION BY THE HUMBLE OIL EMPLOYEES. THE OBJECTIONS TO MR. MINGRAM'S DECLARATION ARE OVERRULED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR # 7077

ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 220, DEFENDANT UNION CARBIDE CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. ARGUED AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: DEFENDANT UNION CARBIDE CORPORATION MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED AS TO ISSUES 1-4 AND 6-10 (SOPHISTICATED INTERMEDIARY AND BULK SUPPLIER DEFENSES AND PUNITIVE DAMAGES) AND TAKEN OFF CALENDAR AS TO ISSUE 5 (FALSE REPRESENTATION). THE SOPHISTICATED INTERMEDIARY DEFENSE DOES NOT SHIELD DEFENDANT FROM LIABILITY. (STEWART). DEFENDANT FAILED TO PRESENT EVIDENCE SHOWING WHAT KNOWLEDGE HAMILTON MATERIALS HAD ABOUT THE HAZARDS OF ASBESTOS AND WHAT WARNINGS, IF ANY, IT PROVIDED TO HAMILTON MATERIALS. THE BULK SUPPLIER DEFENSE IS NOT AVAILABLE TO A SUPPLIER OF RAW ASBESTOS IN ASBESTOS EXPOSURE CASES BECAUSE ASBESTOS IS INHERENTLY DANGEROUS AND RAW ASBESTOS FIBERS DO NOT CHANGE WHEN THEY BECOME A COMPONENT OF AN ASBESTOS-CONTAINING PRODUCT. (GARZA; JENKINS; ARENA). AS TO PUNITIVE DAMAGES, THERE ARE TRIABLE ISSUES WHETHER DEFENDANT MADE MISREPRESENTATIONS ABOUT AND/OR WITHHELD MATERIAL INFORMATION CONCERNING ITS KNOWLEDGE OF THE HAZARDS OF ASBESTOS AND THUS WHETHER IT ACTED IN CONSCIOUS DISREGARD FOR THE SAFETY OF PERSONS WHO WERE EXPOSED TO ITS ASBESTOS. PLAINTIFFS DISMISSED THEIR CAUSE OF ACTION FOR FALSE REPRESENTATION. PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE AN ORDER. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR # 7077

APPEARANCES:
Thomas J. Moses, Esq. of BRYDON HUGO & PARKER (415-808-0300), appearing for defendant / moving party Union Carbide Corporation
Oren P. Noah, Esq. of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Kenneth McGrew

RULING:
DEFENDANT UNION CARBIDE CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Union Carbide Corporation motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1-4 and 6-10 (sophisticated intermediary and bulk supplier defenses and punitive damages) and taken off calendar as to issue 5 (false representation). The sophisticated intermediary defense does not shield defendant from liability. (Stewart). Defendant failed to present evidence showing what knowledge Hamilton Materials had about the hazards of asbestos and what warnings, if any, it provided to Hamilton Materials. The bulk supplier defense is not available to a supplier of raw asbestos in asbestos exposure cases because asbestos is inherently dangerous and raw asbestos fibers do not change when they become a component of an asbestos-containing product. (Garza; Jenkins; Arena). As to punitive damages, there are triable issues whether defendant made misrepresentations about and/or withheld material information concerning its knowledge of the hazards of asbestos and thus whether it acted in conscious disregard for the safety of persons who were exposed to its asbestos. Plaintiffs dismissed their cause of action for false representation. Prevailing party is to prepare an order. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)

James P. Nevin, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Kenneth McGrew

RULING:
DEFENDANT CAMPBELL SOUP COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Campbell Soup Company's motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issue 1 (duty) and granted as to issue 2 (punitive damages). The deposition and declaration of Mr. Cantley creates a triable fact whether Mr. McGrew was exposed to asbestos-containing debris disturbed by defendant. Defendant did not satisfy its burden of production on the issue of its actual or constructive knowledge of asbestos hazards due to its failure to propound contention discovery regarding its constructive knowledge. Every party who disturbs asbestos-containing debris in the vicinity of an individual has a duty to use reasonable care not to cause or contribute to an asbestos-related illness of the individual as a result of the party's disturbing the asbestos-containing debris. Plaintiffs appear to concede the lack of merit of their request for punitive damages. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)

AMENDED MOTION And Notice Of Motion To Compel Plaintiff Bruce Mcgrew To Further Respond To (1) Special Interrogatories; (2) Request For Production; And (3) Form Interrogatories
Off Calendar Sep-25-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Andrew Chew, Atty. for Plaintiff and moving party: Kenneth McGrew
No appearances made by Defendant.

RULING: TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: Defendant Exxon Mobil Corporation's Motion To Compel Plaintiff To Further Respond To Special Interrogatories, Request For Production, And Form Interrogatories is moot as supplemental responses have been served. The court will not determine the adequacy of those responses on this motion. Counsel must meet and confer. However, counsel are advised that responses that refer to and incorporate other responses or depositions are inadequate and would not be approved by the court. Additionally, the reservation of a right to amend or supplement responses is improper. The responses must be full and complete at the time they are made. Any amended responses must be made in compliance with C.C.P. Section 2030.310. Interrogatories require responses made upon reasonable efforts to obtain the information after inquiry to other persons or organizations. Defendant's request for sanctions is denied.
